THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 4 with payroll of 127,051; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC
JCC-GL-004

| **CLASSIFICATION AND TERRITORY ENTRIES ARE USED FOR RATING AND DO NOT DETERMINE COVERAGE BY THEMSELVES. THE APPLICABLE COVERAGE PART, LIMIT, EXCLUSION, AND ENDORSEMENT MUST BE READ WITH THE DECLARATIONS.**<br><br>Territory 003 and Territory 007 carry distinct base rates reflecting the regional risk profile. Operations conducted outside these designated territories must be reported to Juniper Crown Casualty within thirty days.<br><br>Class code 10010 for Mercantile - food or beverage stores applies specifically to location 1. The exposure basis of gross sales excludes collected sales tax and customer returns.<br><br>Class code 41670 for Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages is assigned to location 2. The products-completed operations rate of 0.160 is applied separately from the premises operations rate.<br><br>Reclassification may occur if the nature of your business operations changes during the policy period. Notification to Lumen Field Brokerage is required to ensure accurate rating and continuous protection.<br><br>References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.<br><br>Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.<br><br>If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.<br><br>The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.<br><br>Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.<br><br>Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.<br><br>When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.<br><br>**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**<br>The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 6 with gross sales of 312,398; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.<br><br>Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.<br><br>A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.<br><br>**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

| **THIS BILLING SUMMARY DETAILS THE ADVANCE PREMIUM CHARGES FOR THE COMMERCIAL GENERAL LIABILITY COVERAGE PART ISSUED TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC.**<br><br>The premium for location 1, classified under Mercantile - food or beverage stores, is calculated at $3,412 based on an estimated gross sales exposure of 87,042.<br><br>Location 2, operating as Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages, generates an advance premium of $5,546 derived from the 107,692 gross sales estimate.<br><br>Subcontracted work under class code 16916 at location 3 results in a premium of $2,830, while the concrete construction operations at location 4 produce a premium of $9,452.<br><br>The total advance premium is payable to Juniper Crown Casualty according to the installment schedule selected by Lumen Field Brokerage.<br><br>References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.<br><br>Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.<br><br>If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.<br><br>The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.<br><br>Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.<br><br>Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.<br><br>When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.<br><br>**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**<br><br>The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 3 with cost of work of 431,806; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.<br><br>Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.<br><br>A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.<br><br>**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br><br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

<br>

THIS CONTRACT APPLIES SEPARATELY TO EACH ENTITY DESIGNATED AS AN INSURED. HOWEVER, THIS SEPARATION DOES NOT INCREASE THE EACH OCCURRENCE OR GENERAL AGGREGATE LIMITS ESTABLISHED FOR POLICY LG695378.

When multiple insureds are named in a single suit, our duty to provide a defense remains capped by the scheduled limits. We will coordinate a unified defense unless a direct conflict of interest is identified.

Employees and volunteer workers are considered insureds only while performing duties related to the business of Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC.

A form listed for an exposure item may apply to only the scheduled operation, location, or completed-operations hazard shown in the declarations.

Any breach of conditions by one insured does not automatically void the protection for other insureds, provided the other insureds did not participate in or have prior knowledge of the breach.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 61217 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 61217 at Location 6 with area of 484,915; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

THIS SECTION ESTABLISHES THE PROCEDURES FOR PROCESSING PREMIUM ADJUSTMENTS FOLLOWING THE COMPLETION OF THE ANNUAL EXPOSURE AUDIT.

Upon expiration of the 04/01/2026 to 04/01/2027 policy period, an auditor will verify the actual gross sales, cost of work, and payroll figures.

If the audited exposures exceed the initial estimates of 87,042 for location 1 or 127,051 for location 4, an additional premium invoice will be generated.

Conversely, if the verified exposures fall below the estimates, a return premium will be credited to your account, subject to the established minimum retained premium.

Audit invoices are due within thirty days of the billing date. Disputed audit findings must be submitted in writing with supporting documentation prior to the due date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 47103 at Location 2 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES

The declarations identify class code 47103 at Location 2 with payroll of 500,778; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 18435 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 18435 at Location 3 with gross sales of 597,485; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS




Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                     JCC-GL-004

#### **THIS CONTRACT APPLIES SEPARATELY TO EACH ENTITY DESIGNATED AS AN INSURED. HOWEVER, THIS SEPARATION DOES NOT INCREASE THE EACH OCCURRENCE OR GENERAL AGGREGATE LIMITS ESTABLISHED FOR POLICY LG695378.**

When multiple insureds are named in a single suit, our duty to provide a defense remains capped by the scheduled limits. We will coordinate a unified defense unless a direct conflict of interest is identified.

Employees and volunteer workers are considered insureds only while performing duties related to the business of Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC.

A form listed for an exposure item may apply to only the scheduled operation, location, or completed-operations hazard shown in the declarations.

Any breach of conditions by one insured does not automatically void the protection for other insureds, provided the other insureds did not participate in or have prior knowledge of the breach.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 47103 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail page controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

#### **SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**
The declarations identify class code 47103 at Location 4 with payroll of 620,198; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

#### **SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

#### **SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS**

CLASSIFICATION AND TERRITORY ENTRIES ARE USED FOR RATING AND DO NOT DETERMINE COVERAGE BY THEMSELVES. THE APPLICABLE COVERAGE PART, LIMIT, EXCLUSION, AND ENDORSEMENT MUST BE READ WITH THE DECLARATIONS.

Territory 003 and Territory 007 carry distinct base rates reflecting the regional risk profile. Operations conducted outside these designated territories must be reported to Juniper Crown Casualty within thirty days.

Class code 10010 for Mercantile - food or beverage stores applies specifically to location 1. The exposure basis of gross sales excludes collected sales tax and customer returns.

Class code 41670 for Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages is assigned to location 2. The products-completed operations rate of 0.160 is applied separately from the premises operations rate.

Reclassification may occur if the nature of your business operations changes during the policy period. Notification to Lumen Field Brokerage is required to ensure accurate rating and continuous protection.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.


A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 3 with cost of work of 115,531; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

THIS BILLING SUMMARY DETAILS THE ADVANCE PREMIUM CHARGES FOR THE COMMERCIAL GENERAL LIABILITY COVERAGE PART ISSUED TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC.

The premium for location 1, classified under Mercantile - food or beverage stores, is calculated at $3,412 based on an estimated gross sales exposure of 87,042.

Location 2, operating as Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages, generates an advance premium of $5,546 derived from the 107,692 gross sales estimate.

Subcontracted work under class code 16916 at location 3 results in a premium of $2,830, while the concrete construction operations at location 4 produce a premium of $9,452.

The total advance premium is payable to Juniper Crown Casualty according to the installment schedule selected by Lumen Field Brokerage.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

***

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 5 with cost of work of 222,062; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

If Juniper Crown Casualty elects to cancel this contract for nonpayment of premium, a written notice will be mailed to the named insured at 19 Briar Harbor, Burlington, VT 05401, at least fifteen days prior to the effective date.

THIS SECTION ESTABLISHES THE PROCEDURES FOR PROCESSING PREMIUM ADJUSTMENTS FOLLOWING THE COMPLETION OF THE ANNUAL EXPOSURE AUDIT.

Upon expiration of the 04/01/2026 to 04/01/2027 policy period, an auditor will verify the actual gross sales, cost of work, and payroll figures.

If the audited exposures exceed the initial estimates of 87,042 for location 1 or 127,051 for location 4, an additional premium invoice will be generated.

Conversely, if the verified exposures fall below the estimates, a return premium will be credited to your account, subject to the established minimum retained premium.

Audit invoices are due within thirty days of the billing date. Disputed audit findings must be submitted in writing with supporting documentation prior to the due date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 61217 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES

The declarations identify class code 61217 at Location 4 with area of 373,406; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                             JCC-BIL-02

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 47103 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

The declarations identify class code 47103 at Location 6 with payroll of 397,991; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

ITEM,CLASS / TERRITORY
CGL-0033,10010 / 012
EXPOSURE,LIMIT
Gross sales 509,869,Products-Completed Operations Aggregate: $2,000,000
PREMIUM,FORM
$12,491,CG 21 67 12 19

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

THIS BILLING SUMMARY DETAILS THE ADVANCE PREMIUM CHARGES FOR THE COMMERCIAL GENERAL LIABILITY COVERAGE PART ISSUED TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC.

The premium for location 1, classified under Mercantile - food or beverage stores, is calculated at $3,412 based on an estimated gross sales exposure of 87,042.

Location 2, operating as Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages, generates an advance premium of $5,546 derived from the 107,692 gross sales estimate.

Subcontracted work under class code 16916 at location 3 results in a premium of $2,830, while the concrete construction operations at location 4 produce a premium of $9,452.

The total advance premium is payable to Juniper Crown Casualty according to the installment schedule selected by Lumen Field Brokerage.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 10010 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES

The declarations identify class code 10010 at Location 3 with gross sales of 509,869; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                JCC-BIL-01

**THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.**

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

---

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**
The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 4 with gross sales of 538,089; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS**

| **THIS BILLING SUMMARY DETAILS THE ADVANCE PREMIUM CHARGES FOR THE COMMERCIAL GENERAL LIABILITY COVERAGE PART ISSUED TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC.**<br><br>The premium for location 1, classified under Mercantile - food or beverage stores, is calculated at $3,412 based on an estimated gross sales exposure of 87,042.<br><br>Location 2, operating as Restaurants - with sales of alcoholic beverages, generates an advance premium of $5,546 derived from the 107,692 gross sales estimate.<br><br>Subcontracted work under class code 16916 at location 3 results in a premium of $2,830, while the concrete construction operations at location 4 produce a premium of $9,452.<br><br>The total advance premium is payable to Juniper Crown Casualty according to the installment schedule selected by Lumen Field Brokerage.<br><br>References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 18435 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.<br><br>Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.<br><br>If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.<br><br>The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.<br><br>Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.<br><br>Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.<br><br>When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.<br><br>**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**<br><br>The declarations identify class code 18435 at Location 3 with gross sales of 597,485; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.<br><br>Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.<br><br>A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.<br><br>**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br><br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

THIS CONTRACT APPLIES SEPARATELY TO EACH ENTITY DESIGNATED AS AN INSURED. HOWEVER, THIS SEPARATION DOES NOT INCREASE THE EACH OCCURRENCE OR GENERAL AGGREGATE LIMITS ESTABLISHED FOR POLICY LG695378.

When multiple insureds are named in a single suit, our duty to provide a defense remains capped by the scheduled limits. We will coordinate a unified defense unless a direct conflict of interest is identified.

Employees and volunteer workers are considered insureds only while performing duties related to the business of Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC.

A form listed for an exposure item may apply to only the scheduled operation, location, or completed-operations hazard shown in the declarations.

Any breach of conditions by one insured does not automatically void the protection for other insureds, provided the other insureds did not participate in or have prior knowledge of the breach.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 47103 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

---

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 47103 at Location 4 with payroll of 620,198; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

| **THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.**<br><br>The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.<br><br>The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.<br><br>The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.<br><br>The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.<br><br>References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 10010 at Location 3 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.<br><br>Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.<br><br>If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.<br><br>The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.<br><br>Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.<br><br>Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.<br><br>When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.<br><br>**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**<br>The declarations identify class code 10010 at Location 3 with gross sales of 187,369; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.<br><br>Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.<br><br>A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.<br><br>**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY ISSUES THIS CONTRACT TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC. THIS SECTION OUTLINES THE FOUNDATIONAL ADMINISTRATIVE RULES GOVERNING POLICY NUMBER LG695378.

Premium calculations rely on the estimated exposures provided at inception. The initial charge is provisional and subject to retrospective adjustment once the actual exposure base is verified.

Inspections may be conducted at our discretion at any scheduled premises, including locations 1 through 4. These surveys are strictly for underwriting purposes and do not constitute a warranty of safety or compliance with regulations.

Transfer of your duties or rights under this contract requires our written consent. In the event of the named insured's death, rights transfer automatically to the legal representative handling the estate.

All modifications to the terms must be executed through an official endorsement issued by Lumen Field Brokerage or our authorized underwriting department.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 5 with cost of work of 222,062; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

***

**THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.**

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 1 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 1 with cost of work of 320,348; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS**

***

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 47103 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES

The declarations identify class code 47103 at Location 6 with payroll of 397,991; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES

The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

THIS CONTRACT APPLIES SEPARATELY TO EACH ENTITY DESIGNATED AS AN INSURED. HOWEVER, THIS SEPARATION DOES NOT INCREASE THE EACH OCCURRENCE OR GENERAL AGGREGATE LIMITS ESTABLISHED FOR POLICY LG695378.

When multiple insureds are named in a single suit, our duty to provide a defense remains capped by the scheduled limits. We will coordinate a unified defense unless a direct conflict of interest is identified.

Employees and volunteer workers are considered insureds only while performing duties related to the business of Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC.

A form listed for an exposure item may apply to only the scheduled operation, location, or completed-operations hazard shown in the declarations.

Any breach of conditions by one insured does not automatically void the protection for other insureds, provided the other insureds did not participate in or have prior knowledge of the breach.

The premium for location 1, classified under Mercantile - food or beverage stores, is calculated at $3,412 based on an estimated gross sales exposure of 87,042.

**THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.**

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY ISSUES THIS CONTRACT TO JUNIPER GATE LEARNING CENTER, LLC. THIS SECTION OUTLINES THE FOUNDATIONAL ADMINISTRATIVE RULES GOVERNING POLICY NUMBER LG695378.

Premium calculations rely on the estimated exposures provided at inception. The initial charge is provisional and subject to retrospective adjustment once the actual exposure base is verified.

Inspections may be conducted at our discretion at any scheduled premises, including locations 1 through 4. These surveys are strictly for underwriting purposes and do not constitute a warranty of safety or compliance with regulations.

Transfer of your duties or rights under this contract requires our written consent. In the event of the named insured's death, rights transfer automatically to the legal representative handling the estate.

All modifications to the terms must be executed through an official endorsement issued by Lumen Field Brokerage or our authorized underwriting department.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 10010 at Location 1 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 10010 at Location 1 with gross sales of 87,042; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                         JCC-GL-001

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 4 with payroll of 127,051; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

THIS DOCUMENT SERVES AS FORMAL NOTIFICATION REGARDING THE MATERIALITY OF ENDORSEMENTS ATTACHED TO YOUR COMMERCIAL GENERAL LIABILITY POLICY.

Endorsement GL-EN-003, effective 03/20/2026, introduces a Material change to your risk profile by restricting certain data privacy exposures.

The Employment-Related Practices Exclusion is classified as Potentially Material. You are advised to review your internal human resources policies to mitigate associated risks.

Endorsement GL-EN-001, effective 01/20/2026, is designated as No Material Change. It clarifies existing provisions without altering the fundamental scope of the contract.

Please direct any questions regarding these materiality classifications to your representative at Lumen Field Brokerage.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 5 with cost of work of 222,062; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

THE LIMITS DISPLAYED ON THE DECLARATIONS PAGE REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY ASSUMED BY JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY. THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.

The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.

The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. This per-event cap is subject to the remaining balance of the applicable aggregate limit.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 applies separately to offenses such as libel, slander, or copyright infringement. Payments under this provision reduce the General Aggregate limit.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 16916 at Location 1 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 16916 at Location 1 with cost of work of 320,348; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS
