| **THIS ENDORSEMENT ATTACHES TO POLICY LG695378 AND REMOVES PROTECTION FOR LIABILITIES ARISING OUT OF SPECIFIC EMPLOYMENT-RELATED ACTIONS.**<br><br>No coverage is available for claims alleging wrongful refusal to employ a person, improper termination of employment, or hostile work environment.<br><br>Furthermore, allegations of coercion, demotion, reassignment, discipline, defamation, or harassment directed at an employee are strictly excluded.<br><br>This exclusion applies whether the insured may be held liable as an employer or in any other capacity, and regardless of whether the offense is alleged to have occurred before, during, or after the employment relationship.<br><br>The restriction extends to any claims brought by the spouse, child, parent, or sibling of the affected employee as a consequence of the employment-related practice.<br><br>References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 97047 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.<br><br>Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.<br><br>If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.<br><br>The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.<br><br>Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.<br><br>Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.<br><br>When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.<br><br>**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**<br>The declarations identify class code 97047 at Location 5 with area of 130,133; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.<br><br>Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.<br><br>A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.<br><br>**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

***

### SILICA OR SILICA-RELATED DUST EXCLUSION

Endorsement No. GL-EN-010                                                     Effective 10/20/2026

| | |
| :--- | :--- |
| ITEM<br>**CGL-0010** | CLASS / TERRITORY<br>**41670 / 007** |
| EXPOSURE<br>**Gross sales 200,180** | LIMIT<br>**Personal and Advertising Injury: $1,000,000** |
| PREMIUM<br>**$14,893** | FORM<br>**CG 20 37 09 21** |
| ENDORSEMENT<br>**GL-EN-010** | EFFECTIVE DATE<br>**10/20/2026** |
| MATERIALITY<br>**No Material Change** | |

***

**THIS ENDORSEMENT MODIFIES THE COVERAGE PARAMETERS FOR OPERATIONS CONDUCTED AT LOCATION 2 AND ALL OTHER SCHEDULED PREMISES.**

The contract does not apply to any liability arising directly or indirectly from the inhalation of, ingestion of, or prolonged exposure to silica or silica-related dust.

Additionally, we will not provide a defense or indemnify any insured for costs associated with the abatement, mitigation, or removal of silica from any structure or environment.

This restriction is absolute and applies whether the exposure occurred during ongoing operations or falls within the products-completed operations hazard.

The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 cannot be accessed for any judgments or settlements stemming from silica-related litigation.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

**SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES**
The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 4 with gross sales of 200,180; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

**SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS**

***

Authorized Representative                                                      Countersignature Date

***

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                              CG 20 37

| **THIS ENDORSEMENT ATTACHES TO POLICY LG695378 AND REMOVES PROTECTION FOR LIABILITIES ARISING OUT OF SPECIFIC EMPLOYMENT-RELATED ACTIONS.** | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued. |
| No coverage is available for claims alleging wrongful refusal to employ a person, improper termination of employment, or hostile work environment. | Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records. |
| Furthermore, allegations of coercion, demotion, reassignment, discipline, defamation, or harassment directed at an employee are strictly excluded. | When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together. |
| This exclusion applies whether the insured may be held liable as an employer or in any other capacity, and regardless of whether the offense is alleged to have occurred before, during, or after the employment relationship. | **SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES** |
| The restriction extends to any claims brought by the spouse, child, parent, or sibling of the affected employee as a consequence of the employment-related practice. | The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 6 with gross sales of 312,398; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes. |
| References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule. | Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy. |
| Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations. | A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory. |
| If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date. | **SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES** |
| The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries. | The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations. |
| Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit. |
| | When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations. |
| | **SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

| **THIS ENDORSEMENT MODIFIES THE COVERAGE PARAMETERS FOR OPERATIONS CONDUCTED AT LOCATION 2 AND ALL OTHER SCHEDULED PREMISES.** <br><br> The contract does not apply to any liability arising directly or indirectly from the inhalation of, ingestion of, or prolonged exposure to silica or silica-related dust. <br><br> Additionally, we will not provide a defense or indemnify any insured for costs associated with the abatement, mitigation, or removal of silica from any structure or environment. <br><br> This restriction is absolute and applies whether the exposure occurred during ongoing operations or falls within the products-completed operations hazard. <br><br> The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 cannot be accessed for any judgments or settlements stemming from silica-related litigation. <br><br> References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 18435 at Location 5 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule. <br><br> Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations. <br><br> If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date. <br><br> The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries. <br><br> Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. | A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued. <br><br> Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records. <br><br> When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together. <br><br> **SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES** <br> The declarations identify class code 18435 at Location 5 with gross sales of 383,418; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes. <br><br> Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy. <br><br> A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory. <br><br> **SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES** <br> The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations. <br><br> A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit. <br><br> When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations. <br><br> **SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

THIS ENDORSEMENT, DESIGNATED AS GL-EN-003, TAKES EFFECT ON 03/20/2026 AND RESTRICTS THE SCOPE OF THE PERSONAL AND ADVERTISING INJURY COVERAGE.

Protection is completely removed for any claims alleging the unauthorized access to, or disclosure of, confidential or personal information.

This includes, but is not limited to, patents, trade secrets, processing methods, customer lists, financial data, and any other non-public proprietary information.

The restriction applies even if the unauthorized disclosure was accidental or resulted from a failure in your network security protocols.

The Personal and Advertising Injury limit of $1,000,000 remains applicable only to those offenses not explicitly excluded by this or other amendatory documents.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES
The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 4 with payroll of 450,422; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS

__________________________________________________     __________________________________________________
Authorized Representative                             Countersignature Date

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                        CG 24 04

Transfer of your duties or rights under this contract requires our written consent. In the event of the named insured's death, rights transfer automatically to the legal representative handling the estate.

| **JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY IS COMMITTED TO TRANSPARENCY REGARDING THE CALCULATION OF YOUR COMMERCIAL PREMIUMS. THIS NOTICE OUTLINES THE FACTORS INFLUENCING YOUR RATES.** <br><br> Your final premium is heavily dependent on the accurate reporting of exposure bases, including the 87,042 gross sales at location 1 and the 127,051 payroll at location 4. <br><br> The products-completed operations rate varies by classification. For instance, class code 16916 carries a rate of 0.076, while class code 41670 utilizes a rate of 0.160. <br><br> Failure to provide accurate exposure estimates may result in substantial audit balances at the conclusion of the 04/01/2026 to 04/01/2027 policy period. <br><br> This notice is strictly for informational purposes. It does not amend, extend, or alter the provisions of policy number LG695378. <br><br> References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 91560 at Location 6 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule. <br><br> Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations. <br><br> If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date. <br><br> The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries. <br><br> Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy. <br><br> A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued. | Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records. <br><br> When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together. <br><br> **SECTION A - SCHEDULE REFERENCES** <br><br> The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 6 with payroll of 560,644; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes. <br><br> Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy. <br><br> A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory. <br><br> **SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES** <br><br> The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations. <br><br> A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit. <br><br> When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations. <br><br> **SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

<br><br><br>
Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                            JCC-NOT-02

THIS SECTION ESTABLISHES THE PROCEDURES FOR PROCESSING PREMIUM ADJUSTMENTS FOLLOWING THE COMPLETION OF THE ANNUAL EXPOSURE AUDIT.

Upon expiration of the 04/01/2026 to 04/01/2027 policy period, an auditor will verify the actual gross sales, cost of work, and payroll figures.

If the audited exposures exceed the initial estimates of 87,042 for location 1 or 127,051 for location 4, an additional premium invoice will be generated.

Conversely, if the verified exposures fall below the estimates, a return premium will be credited to your account, subject to the established minimum retained premium.

Audit invoices are due within thirty days of the billing date. Disputed audit findings must be submitted in writing with supporting documentation prior to the due date.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 2 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

### COLUMN 1

THIS AMENDATORY ENDORSEMENT MODIFIES THE CANCELLATION AND NONRENEWAL CONDITIONS TO COMPLY WITH THE STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS OF THE STATE OF VERMONT.

If Juniper Crown Casualty elects to cancel this contract for nonpayment of premium, a written notice will be mailed to the named insured at 19 Briar Harbor, Burlington, VT 05401, at least fifteen days prior to the effective date.

For cancellation reasons other than nonpayment, a forty-five day advance written notice is required. The notice will state the specific factual basis for the termination.

In the event of nonrenewal, we will provide written notice to Lumen Field Brokerage and the named insured no less than forty-five days before the expiration date of 04/01/2027.

Any provisions in the base contract that conflict with Vermont insurance statutes are hereby superseded by this state-specific amendatory document.

THESE CAPS APPLY REGARDLESS OF THE NUMBER OF INSUREDS, CLAIMS FILED, OR PERSONS INJURED.**<br><br>The General Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 is the total cap for all covered events during the policy period, except those falling under the products-completed operations hazard.<br><br>The Products-Completed Operations Aggregate limit of $2,000,000 functions independently. It caps the total responsibility for incidents arising from your completed work or distributed goods.<br><br>The Each Occurrence limit of $1,000,000 restricts the payout for any single event. [Left Column]

THIS CONTRACT APPLIES SEPARATELY TO EACH ENTITY DESIGNATED AS AN INSURED. HOWEVER, THIS SEPARATION DOES NOT INCREASE THE EACH OCCURRENCE OR GENERAL AGGREGATE LIMITS ESTABLISHED FOR POLICY LG695378.

When multiple insureds are named in a single suit, our duty to provide a defense remains capped by the scheduled limits. We will coordinate a unified defense unless a direct conflict of interest is identified.

Employees and volunteer workers are considered insureds only while performing duties related to the business of Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC.

A form listed for an exposure item may apply to only the scheduled operation, location, or completed-operations hazard shown in the declarations.

Any breach of conditions by one insured does not automatically void the protection for other insureds, provided the other insureds did not participate in or have prior knowledge of the breach.

References to classification, territory, and exposure basis include class code 41670 at Location 4 only when that operation appears in the declarations or rating schedule.

Headings are included for ease of reading and do not alter limits, exclusions, additional-insured status, completed-operations treatment, or premium calculations.

If a form schedule and an endorsement detail page address the same operation, the endorsement detail controls from its effective date.

The insured should retain sales records, payroll records, contracts, leases, certificates, and location records used to support the exposure entries.

Coverage summaries on one page do not replace the limits schedule, exposure schedule, forms schedule, or exclusion endorsement attached to the policy.

[Right Column]

A certificate, binder note, invoice, or producer correspondence item does not amend an exposure or additional-insured entry unless an endorsement is issued.

Final premium may require allocation of sales, payroll, area, contract cost, territory, products-completed operations, and other exposure records.

When a class code appears on multiple schedules, the item identifier, location, form number, and effective date should be used to read the entries together.

The declarations identify class code 41670 at Location 4 with gross sales of 200,180; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

A location, project, classification, or exposure entry does not create coverage for operations that are otherwise excluded or outside the policy territory.

SECTION B - LIMITS AND AGGREGATES
The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.

A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.

When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.

JUNIPER CROWN CASUALTY IS COMMITTED TO TRANSPARENCY REGARDING THE CALCULATION OF YOUR COMMERCIAL PREMIUMS. THIS NOTICE OUTLINES THE FACTORS INFLUENCING YOUR RATES.

Your final premium is heavily dependent on the accurate reporting of exposure bases, including the 87,042 gross sales at location 1 and the 127,051 payroll at location 4.

The products-completed operations rate varies by classification. For instance, class code 16916 carries a rate of 0.076, while class code 41670 utilizes a rate of 0.160.

Failure to provide accurate exposure estimates may result in substantial audit balances at the conclusion of the 04/01/2026 to 04/01/2027 policy period.

This notice is strictly for informational purposes. It does not amend, extend, or alter the provisions of policy number LG695378.

The declarations identify class code 91560 at Location 6 with payroll of 235,736; that entry is used for rating and schedule reference purposes.

Coverage applies only as provided by the applicable coverage form, limits schedule, exclusion endorsements, and any additional-insured endorsement attached to the policy.

LIMITS AND AGGREGATES**<br>The each occurrence, damage to premises rented to you, medical expense, personal and advertising injury, products-completed operations, and general aggregate limits apply as stated in the declarations.<br><br>A sublimit, deductible, self-insured retention, or endorsement limit is part of the applicable limit and does not increase that limit.<br><br>When more than one scheduled operation could apply to a claim, the most specific applicable form and endorsement must be read with the declarations.<br><br>**SECTION C - ADDITIONAL INSUREDS AND CONTRACTS** |

Juniper Gate Learning Center, LLC                                                      JCC-GL-005
